name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
فَمَا لَهُمْ عَنِ ٱلتَّذْكِرَةِ مُعْرِضِينَ (٤٩)
```

```english
What is the matter with them that they evade the Reminder (49)
```

```arabic
كَأَنَّهُمْ حُمُرٌ مُّسْتَنفِرَةٌ (٥٠)
```

```english
as if they were terrified asses (50)
```

```arabic
فَرَّتْ مِن قَسْوَرَةٍۭ (٥١)
```

```english
fleeing from a lion? (51)
```

```arabic
بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُمْ أَن يُؤْتَىٰ صُحُفًا مُّنَشَّرَةً (٥٢)
```

```english
But everyone of them desires to be given unrolled scriptures [from Allah]! (52)
```

```arabic
كَلَّا ۖ بَل لَّا يَخَافُونَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ (٥٣)
```

```english
No! Indeed, they do not fear the Hereafter. (53)
```

```arabic
كَلَّآ إِنَّهُۥ تَذْكِرَةٌ (٥٤)
```

```english
No! It is indeed a reminder. (54)
```

```arabic
فَمَن شَآءَ ذَكَرَهُۥ (٥٥)
```

```english
So let anyone who wishes be mindful of it. (55)
```

```arabic
وَمَا يَذْكُرُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ هُوَ أَهْلُ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ وَأَهْلُ ٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ (٥٦)
```

```english
And they will not be mindful unless Allah wishes. He is worthy of [your] being wary [of Him] and He is worthy to forgive. (56)
```

## (Commentary)

In the sense of drawing a conclusion from the preceding warnings and promises, the discourse is presented in the form of wonder at their turning away from the reminder of the Quran and their aversion to the plain truth. It is as if it were said: Since the matter is such, it is incumbent upon them to respond to the call of truth and to take heed through the reminder; thus, it is a matter of wonder that they are turning away from it. No indeed! Rather, they do not believe in the Message, and every man among them desires that a book from Allah be sent down specifically to him. No indeed! Rather, they do not fear the Hereafter, and thus they are not deterred by any warning.

Then, He offers the reminder to them as a proposal, such that they have the choice to accept or reject; if they wish, they may accept, and if they wish, they may reject. However, they must know that they are not independent (مستقلين) in their volition, nor can they frustrate Allah, the Glorified. They cannot take heed unless Allah wills, for the decree of destiny (القدر) is absolutely operative concerning them.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَما لَهُمْ عَنِ التَّذْكِرَةِ مُعْرِضِينَ** **"What is the matter with them that they should turn away from the Reminder,"** (74:49) is a derivation from the preceding reminder and exhortation. The interrogation is for expressing wonder. The phrase **لَهُمْ** "with them" relates to a suppressed verb, the estimation being: "What has happened to them?" and **مُعْرِضِينَ** "turning away" is a circumstantial qualifier (حال) for the pronoun in **لَهُمْ**, while **عَنِ التَّذْكِرَةِ** "from the Reminder" relates to "turning away."

The meaning is: Since the matter is such, what thing has occurred—or happened—to the polytheists who deny the reminder of the Quran, such that they are in a state of turning away from it? That is, it was obligatory upon them to believe and have faith, yet they turned away from it, which is a cause for wonder.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **كَأَنَّهُمْ حُمُرٌ مُسْتَنْفِرَةٌ فَرَّتْ مِنْ قَسْوَرَةٍ** **"as if they were startled asses, fleeing from a lion?"** (74:50-51). This is a comparison of them in terms of their state of turning away from the reminder. The word **حُمُرٌ** is the plural of **حمار** (ass), and what is meant here are wild asses. **مُسْتَنْفِرَةٌ** (startled) denotes a state of being spooked or in flight. **قَسْوَرَةٍ** refers to a lion or a hunter; it has been interpreted as both meanings.

The meaning is: they turn away from the reminder as if they were wild asses that have bolted in terror from a lion or a hunter.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً** **"Indeed, every one of them desires to be given unrolled manuscripts."** (74:52). What is meant by "unrolled manuscripts" (صحفاً منشرة) is a heavenly book containing the true call.

The speech contains a transition (إضراب) from the mention of their turning away. The meaning is that their turning away from the reminder is not merely due to aversion; rather, every man among them desires that a book be sent down to him from Allah, containing what the call of the Quran contains.

Attributing this to them is an allusion to their arrogance (استكبار) toward Allah, the Glorified—that they would only accept His call and not reject it if He were to call each one of them by sending down a heavenly book to him independently. As for the call through the medium of Messengership (الرسالة), they will not respond to it, even if it is the truth supported by manifest signs.

Thus, the verse carries the same meaning as what Allah, the Glorified, narrated of their saying: **لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ حَتَّى نُؤْتى مِثْلَ ما أُوتِيَ رُسُلُ اللهِ** **"‘We will not believe until we are given the like of what was given to Allah’s apostles.’"** (6:124), and the meaning of what the nations said to their Messengers: **إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنا** **"‘You are nothing but humans like us,’"** (14:10) based on what we have established regarding their argument for denying the Messengership of the Messengers.

It has been said that the verse carries the meaning of their saying to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم which Allah narrated in His saying: **وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّى تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنا كِتاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ** **"‘and we will not believe your ascension until you bring down to us a book that we may read.’"** (17:93).

However, this is refuted by the fact that the implication of the verse under discussion is that unrolled manuscripts be sent down to *each one* of them individually, distinct from what is sent to others, not the descent of a single book from heaven upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for everyone to read, as is the implication of the verse in Surah al-Isra.

It has also been said: What is meant is the descent of books from heaven to them addressed with their names, commanding them to believe in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

It has further been said: What is meant is that books be sent down to them from heaven granting them immunity from punishment and the completion of favor so that they might believe, otherwise they would remain in their disbelief. Other interpretations have also been mentioned.

All of these are meanings far removed from the context (السياق), and reliance should be placed on what we have previously stated.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **كَلَّا بَلْ لا يَخافُونَ الْآخِرَةَ** **"No indeed! Rather they do not fear the Hereafter."** (74:53). This is a rebuff (ردع) to them regarding their desire for a heavenly book to be sent to each one of them. For the call of Messengership is supported by manifest signs and definitive proofs that leave no room for doubt for any doubter; thus, the proof (الحجة) is complete and established for the Messenger and others alike, without any need for every person among those called to be given unrolled manuscripts.

Furthermore, Messengership requires a purity of essence (طهارة الذات) and a capacity of the soul (صلاحية النفس) that the souls of other people lack, as is the implication of the response of the Exalted in Surah al-An'am to their saying: **لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ حَتَّى نُؤْتى مِثْلَ ما أُوتِيَ رُسُلُ اللهِ** **"‘We will not believe until we are given the like of what was given to Allah’s apostles.’"** (6:124) with His saying: **اللهُ أَعْلَمُ حَيْثُ يَجْعَلُ رِسالَتَهُ** **"Allah knows best where to place His apostleship."** (6:124).

His saying: **بَلْ لا يَخافُونَ الْآخِرَةَ** **"Rather they do not fear the Hereafter"** is a transition from His saying: **يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ** "every one of them desires..." etc. The meaning is that their proposal for the descent of a book upon every man among them is a superficial claim by which they intend to deflect the call from themselves. The real reason for their disbelief and denial of the call is that they do not fear the Hereafter. Had they feared it, they would have believed and would not have proposed a sign after the proof had been established by the appearance of manifest signs.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **كَلَّا إِنَّهُ تَذْكِرَةٌ** **"No indeed! It is a reminder."** (74:54). This is a second rebuff to their proposal for the descent of a heavenly book for every man among them. The meaning is: We shall not send down such a book; indeed, the Quran is a reminder (تذكرة) and an exhortation by which We admonish them, and We intend nothing more than that. The consequence of this is the recompense We have prepared for the obedient and the disobedient.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَمَنْ شاءَ ذَكَرَهُ** **"So let anyone who wishes take heed of it."** (74:55). That is, whoever wishes may be admonished by it, for it is only a call within the sphere of choice (الاختيار) without any compulsion.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَما يَذْكُرُونَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ هُوَ أَهْلُ التَّقْوى وَأَهْلُ الْمَغْفِرَةِ** **"But they will not take heed unless Allah wishes. He is the source of godwariness and the source of forgiveness."** (74:56). This is a refutation of what they might mistakenly assume from His saying: **فَمَنْ شاءَ ذَكَرَهُ** "So let anyone who wishes take heed of it"—namely, that the matter is entirely up to them and that they are independent (مستقلون) in their volition and the actions resulting from it. Thus, if they did not wish to take heed and did not do so, they would have overcome the Exalted in what He intended and frustrated Him in what He willed regarding their taking heed.

The summary of this refutation is that the decree of destiny (القدر) applies to their actions just as it applies to all other events. Their taking heed—if they do take heed—even though it is an intentional act (فعل اختياري) proceeding from them by their choice without compulsion, the Divine Will (المشية الإلهية) is nonetheless attached to it insofar as it is intentional. This means that Allah the Exalted wills, with a creative will (إرادة تكوينية), that the human being perform such-and-such an act by his own will and choice. Thus, the act is intentional and possible (ممكن) in relation to the human being, while it is simultaneously the object of the Divine Will and necessarily realized (ضروري التحقق) in relation to it; without it, the act would not occur.

His saying: **هُوَ أَهْلُ التَّقْوى وَأَهْلُ الْمَغْفِرَةِ** **"He is the source of godwariness and the source of forgiveness"** means He is worthy of being feared (يتقى منه) because He possesses absolute authority (الولاية المطلقة) over everything, and in His hand lies the happiness and wretchedness of man. He is also worthy of forgiving those who are wary of Him because He is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

The sentence—that is, His saying: **هُوَ أَهْلُ التَّقْوى وَأَهْلُ الْمَغْفِرَةِ**—is suitable as a justification for the preceding call in His saying: **إِنَّهُ تَذْكِرَةٌ فَمَنْ شاءَ ذَكَرَهُ** "It is a reminder. So let anyone who wishes take heed of it," which is apparent. It is also suitable as a justification for His saying: **وَما يَذْكُرُونَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ** "But they will not take heed unless Allah wishes." For His being the source of godwariness (أهل التقوى) and the source of forgiveness (أهل المغفرة) is not realized except by His possessing an effective will (إرادة نافذة) over them that permeates their actions. Thus, they are not left to themselves and their desires such that they could frustrate Allah through their rebellion and arrogance.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *Tafsir al-Qummi*, in a narration from Abu al-Jarud from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of the Exalted: **بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً** "Indeed, every one of them desires to be given unrolled manuscripts." He said: "That was because they said: 'O Muhammad, it has reached us that when a man from the Children of Israel committed a sin, he would wake up to find his sin and its expiation written at his headrest.'"

Then Gabriel descended to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "Your people are asking you for the custom of the Children of Israel regarding sins. If they wish, We shall do that to them, but We shall seize them as We used to seize the Children of Israel." They claimed that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم disliked that for his people.

**I say:** The story does not harmonize with the tone of the verse, and the narration is not devoid of an indication toward the weakness of the story.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, it is narrated by 'Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn Jarir, and Ibn al-Mundhir from al-Suddi from Abu Salih, who said: "They said: 'If Muhammad is truthful, let there appear under the head of every man among us a manuscript containing his acquittal and security from the Fire.' Then it was revealed: **بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً** 'Indeed, every one of them desires to be given unrolled manuscripts.'"

**I say:** The context of the verses and the rebuff contained therein do not harmonize with this story.

In the same source, it is narrated by 'Abd ibn Humayd and Ibn al-Mundhir from Mujahid regarding **بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً** "Indeed, every one of them desires to be given unrolled manuscripts." He said: "[Addressed] to so-and-so, son of so-and-so, from the Lord of the Worlds—a manuscript would be placed at the head of every man when he wakes up, which he would read."

**I say:** What is in this narration can be reconciled with the previous narration and with the meaning of the verse we have presented.

In the same source, it is narrated by 'Abd ibn Humayd and Ibn al-Mundhir from Qatadah regarding the saying of the Exalted: **بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً** "Indeed, every one of them desires to be given unrolled manuscripts." He said: "Some people said to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: 'If it would please you that we follow you, then bring us a book specifically for us, commanding us to follow you.'"

**I say:** This narration is applicable to the interpretation of the verse stating that it carries the meaning of His saying: **وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ** "and we will not believe your ascension..." (17:93), and we have already discussed the problems with that.

In *Tafsir al-Qummi*, regarding the saying of the Exalted: **هُوَ أَهْلُ التَّقْوى وَأَهْلُ الْمَغْفِرَةِ** "He is the source of godwariness and the source of forgiveness." He said: "He is worthy of being feared and worthy of forgiving."

In *al-Tawhid*, with its chain of narration to Abu Basir from Abu 'Abdillah (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **هُوَ أَهْلُ التَّقْوى وَأَهْلُ الْمَغْفِرَةِ** "He is the source of godwariness and the source of forgiveness." He said: "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'I am worthy of being feared—that My servant should not associate anything with Me—and I am worthy, if My servant does not associate anything with Me, to admit him into Paradise.'"

And he said: "Indeed, Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, has sworn by His Might and Majesty that He will not punish the people of His monotheism (أهل توحيده) with the Fire."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, it is narrated by Ibn Mardawayh from 'Abdullah ibn Dinar, who said: "I heard Abu Hurayrah, Ibn 'Umar, and Ibn 'Abbas saying: 'The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked about the saying of Allah: **هُوَ أَهْلُ التَّقْوى وَأَهْلُ الْمَغْفِرَةِ** "He is the source of godwariness and the source of forgiveness." He said: "Allah says: I am worthy of being feared—so that no partner is set up with Me. If I am feared and no partner is set up with Me, then I am worthy of forgiving whatever is besides that."'"

**I say:** There are several narrations from him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم carrying this same meaning.
